channel.title: UK Public Affairs & Lobbying Jobs
channel.description: Job listings for Public Affairs roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Senior Account Executive (Corporate)
item.description: The Organisation ‚Äç We are partnering with a fast-growing global agency, headquartered in London, known for its work that has significant client impact and value. They are looking to further expand their Corporate team after a series of new business wins and bring in a Senior Account Executive at an exciting time. This team delivers campaigns that reach the right audiences, strengthen reputation and are completely sector-agnostic. The agency is recognised for its strong commitment to diversity and inclusion across the sector. With a collaborative culture and a clear focus on professional development, this is an excellent environment to learn, grow, and make an impact. ‚Äç The Opportunity ‚Äç As Senior Account Executive, you‚Äôll support the running of several high-profile corporate accounts and campaigns, helping to deliver effective and strategic communications programmes. You‚Äôll act as a trusted day-to-day contact for your team, manage delivery across multiple workstreams, and ensure high-quality outputs. You will also have the opportunity to contribute ideas to help further establish the practice and its capabilities. ‚Äç Main Responsibilities Support day-to-day client relationships, providing accurate, timely and well-considered advice. Build and maintain relationships with journalists, pitch stories, handle media inquiries, arrange interviews, and provide media training. Monitor media coverage, collate results, and create performance reports for clients and teams. Help deliver integrated corporate communications activity, including issues and reputation programmes. Coordinate elements of project delivery, ensuring deadlines, quality standards and briefs are met. Contribute to new business pitches and support organic growth across existing accounts. Assist with managing workflows, budgets and resourcing, escalating risks where appropriate. Play an active role in supporting the agency‚Äôs inclusive culture and wider industry initiatives. Skills & Experience Experience in corporate communications or reputation management, with agency experience essential ‚Äì you must already be at Senior Account Executive level. Growing confidence in client-facing environments, including exposure to senior stakeholders. Strong organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a fast-paced setting. An interest in the commercial side of agency life, including new business and account development. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with media relations experience desirable. ‚Äç For more details on the client mix and wider package, please apply via the link for a confidential conversation. ‚Äç For more information on communications and public affairs roles with leading agencies, contact Tasmin Khin at Tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Head of Advocacy
item.description: The Opportunity ‚Äç Water UK is looking to recruit a Head of Advocacy, a high-profile leadership role with responsibility for shaping and delivering our national advocacy strategy. ‚Äç Reporting to the Director of Public Affairs, the role sitsat the heart of a rapidly growing, influential team and offers regular exposureto senior political figures, CEOs and FTSE-level industry leaders. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead Water UK‚Äôs advocacy strategy, shaping and delivering campaigns that influence national policy and public debate Provide high-quality briefings for senior stakeholders, and represent Water UK externally, including at party conferences, parliamentary meetings and senior stakeholder roundtables Work closely with the CEO, Director of Public Affairs and Head of Public Affairs to ensure advocacy activity aligns with organisational priorities, and collaborate across teams to deliver consistent messaging and strategy Line-manage and develop an Advocacy Manager contributing to a high-performance culture ‚Äç Key Skills & Experience Required ‚Äç Significant experience in advocacy, government relations or similar senior political roles, with a proven track record of shaping and delivering advocacy or influencing strategies Strong political judgement and deep understanding of UK parliamentary processes, with prior knowledge of the water, environmental or infrastructure sectors being desirable (but not essential) Confident working with senior internal and external stakeholders at the highest level, with the ability to handle high expectations Line management or team leadership experience is highly desirable ‚Äç To apply ‚Äç We have partnered with Ellwood Atfield on the appointment ofthis role. If this role is of interest, please apply with your CV and Covering Letter via the link below - we welcome early applications. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Account Manager / Senior Account Manager (Healthcare & Public Sector)
item.description: The Organisation ‚Äç Our client is an integrated public affairs and communications agency. A certified B Corp, the organisation believes communications should be a force for good, with a commitment to positive outcomes for patients, communities and the wider health and care system. Partof a wider communications group, the agency combines specialist expertise withbroader integrated capability across public affairs, corporate communications and digital. It offers a friendly, supportive culture and a growing presence across regional offices. The Opportunity ‚Äç This role sits within a growing team and will focus ondelivering high-quality PR and media relations programmes across healthcare andpublic sector accounts. The successful candidate will play a central role indriving excellent client delivery and providing trusted counsel across a varied portfolio. We are open to looking at candidates at Account Manager and Senior Account Manager level. The team is open to candidates from agency orin-house backgrounds, including those from wider purpose-led sectors looking to specialise and gain more experience in health and social care. A strong interest or understanding of the UK health landscape is important, alongside solid PR credentials and experience securing impactful coverage. Main Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead day-to-day delivery of PR and media programmes across health and public sector accounts Develop and implement proactive media strategies aligned to client objectives Secure high-quality coverage across local, trade, national and digital media Draft compelling press materials, opinion pieces, briefing documents and social content Manage issues-sensitive communications and support reputation management activity Build and maintain strong relationships with journalists, stakeholders and client teams Monitor media and policy developments, providing timely advice and insight Contribute to new business activity and support the continued growth of the team ‚Äç Skills & Experience ‚Äç Expertise in public relations, ideally within an agency environment but in-house experience will also be considered Strong media relations background with a demonstrable track record of securing coverage Experience across social media, reputation management and integrated communications Ability to operate confidently in fast-paced and issues-sensitive environments Excellent written and verbal communication skills Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple client workstreams A collaborative mindset and interest in specialising within a growing purpose-led practice To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‚Äç For more information on communications roles, contact TasminKhin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to performat their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if yourequire any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Policy Manager
item.description: An established trade within the financial and professional services sector is seeking a Policy Manager to support its commercial finance agenda. The organisation works closely with industry participants, policy makersand regulators to promote a responsible and well-functioning sector that supports UK businesses and the wider economy. With increasing focus on SME access to finance amid economic and regulatory change, this role offers the opportunity to contribute tohigh-profile policy discussions and engage directly with senior stakeholders across industry and government. Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead on policy issues affecting SME access to finance, including areas such as personal guarantees, credit data and sector-specific lending. Draft consultation responses, policy papers, briefings and board materials with minimal oversight. Support research projects, events and wider delivery of the commercial finance policy programme. Coordinate and contribute to member committees, including preparing agendas and minutes. Engage with government, regulators and industry stakeholders through meetings and roundtables. Build relationships with a broad member base, including banks and specialist lenders. Monitor regulatory and legislative developments, identifying implications and engagement opportunities. Produce member communications including updates, articles and presentations. Skills & Experience ‚Äç Excellent written skills and proven ability to write policy reports and consultation documents Previous experience in financial services, policy, government, regulation or a membership body environment. Confident engaging with senior stakeholders and representing organisational positions. Strong analytical and research skills, with the ability to interpret complex regulatory material. Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to performat their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if yourequire any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Senior Account Director (Social)
item.description: The Organisation ‚Äç A social-first creative agency built on the belief that brands must think differently and move fast to stay relevant. Small, ambitious and constantly evolving, the team lives and breathes social: creating bold, culturally attuned work for major consumer-facing brands. While operating with a start-up mentality, the agenc ybenefits from the backing of a larger group, offering stability, infrastructure and opportunity without compromising entrepreneurial spirit. The Opportunity ‚Äç This is a pivotal hire: a Senior Account Director who willact as a right hand to the Founder and play a central role in scaling the business. You will lead from the front - pitching, winning and growing client relationships - while shaping how the agency evolves. The role requires confidence, commercial instinct and thehunger to build. You will thrive in a fast-moving environment where social is brave, immediate and always evolving. Main Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead and grow senior client relationships across major consumer brands Drive new business activity, from chemistry meetings to pitch delivery and conversion Shape strategic responses that reflect a deep understanding of social-first thinking Partner closely with the Founder to scale the agency sustainably and ambitiously Provide confident counsel to clients, reading the room and adapting approach accordingly Collaborate with creative and strategy teams to deliver standout, culturally relevant work Identify opportunities to expand accounts and increase revenue streams Lead the team and mentor juniors ‚Äç Skills & Experience ‚Äç Proven experience at Account Director level and above within a social or integrated agency Strong track record of pitching, winning and growing accounts Commercially astute, with a hunger to scale a business and drive revenue Confident communicator who can influence senior stakeholders and energise a team Entrepreneurial mindset with comfort working in a small, fast-paced environment Deep understanding of social platforms, culture and emerging trends Resilient, adaptable and motivated by building something ambitious ‚Äç This is an opportunity for someone with a start-up mentality and a genuine appetite to help shape the next phase of growth - bringing energy, pace and ambition to a business that believes social should be brave and fast. ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For moreinformation, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to performat their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if yourequire any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: PR Manager
item.description: Support the reputation of a leading asset manager across Europe ‚Äç This role focuses on strengthening the organisation‚Äôs reputation across European media, with particular responsibility for day‚Äëto‚Äëday activity in Spain. Working within the European Media Relations team and closely with colleagues across Corporate Affairs, the PR Manager will help shape how the business is represented in key markets and ensure accurate, timely and proactive communication with journalists and external stakeholders. The position offers the opportunity to work in an international environment, contribute to high‚Äëprofile media activity and support the delivery of a coordinated communications strategy across multiple countries. It is well suited to someone who is confident with media engagement, comfortable working across borders and able to identify strong stories within a complex financial environment. ‚Äç Main responsibilities ‚Äç Contribute to the development and delivery of the media strategy across Continental Europe, supporting projects that highlight the organisation‚Äôs investment capabilities and broader business priorities. Lead day‚Äëto‚Äëday media relations activity in Spain, working with the Senior Media Relations Manager and the local PR agency to identify stories, develop news angles and engage with trade, regional, national and broadcast media. Build strong relationships with journalists and key media stakeholders, ensuring accurate and timely communication. Work with internal teams to identify opportunities and risks, advising on media considerations and supporting the management of reputational issues. Support the development of proactive corporate campaigns across European markets. Draft press releases, briefings, Q&A documents and other communication materials. Organise and attend interviews, visits and events as required. Assist in assessing the wider impact of emerging issues and help develop appropriate responses. Respond to media enquiries and prepare clear lines to take. Work with colleagues across Corporate Affairs to deliver pan‚ÄëEuropean projects and support wider departmental activity. Review media coverage and contribute to evaluation of campaign performance and KPIs. ‚Äç Knowledge, skills and experience ‚Äç Experience in day‚Äëto‚Äëday media relations and confidence engaging with journalists in person, by phone and in writing. Strong communication skills and the ability to draft clear, accurate and engaging content. Fluent in Spanish and English, with the ability to translate short pieces of content. Ability to understand and communicate complex financial and organisational information. Journalistic skills, including identifying stories and drafting longer‚Äëform content. Well organised, able to manage multiple projects and maintain strong attention to detail. Understanding of media measurement and evaluation. Comfortable adapting to changing priorities and working in a fast‚Äëmoving environment. Strong relationship‚Äëbuilding skills and the ability to work effectively with colleagues across different countries. Knowledge of financial services or asset management is helpful, though not essential. Experience working in an international team or environment is desirable. Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director (Public Affairs)
item.description: The Organisation ‚Äç Our client is an independent, boutique communications and public affairs consultancy working with a range of high-profile organisations across corporate, policy and reputational issues. The firm has built a strong reputation for delivering thoughtful, strategic advice and integrated campaigns. The agency is entering an exciting phase of growth and evolution. With an ambitious leadership team and a collaborative culture, it offers the opportunity to shape both client work and the direction of the business as it scales. The Opportunity ‚Äç This is a senior leadership role for an experienced public affairs professional who combines strategic counsel with strong commercial instincts. The Director will play a central role in guiding client programmes, developing new business opportunities and strengthening the agency‚Äôs senior capability. Joining at a pivotal moment in the firm‚Äôs growth, the successful candidate will help deepen client relationships, support the expansion of the agency‚Äôs portfolio and contribute to the development of a high-performing team. The role would suit someone seeking genuine ownership and responsibility within a growing consultancy environment, with the opportunity to influence decisions, shape ways of working, and play an active role in the development of both the team and the wider business. ‚Äç Main Responsibilities ‚Äç Provide senior strategic counsel to clients, developing integrated public affairs and communications strategies that deliver measurable impact Lead and grow key client relationships, acting as a trusted adviser to senior stakeholders Identify and drive new business opportunities, contributing to the agency‚Äôs continued commercial growth Lead and contribute to pitches, proposals and scope development for prospective and existing clients Oversee account delivery to ensure high-quality, insight-led work across public affairs, policy and media engagement Support the development and mentoring of consultants, strengthening capability across the team Monitor political and policy developments to inform client strategies and identify emerging opportunities Contribute to the wider leadership of the business, supporting operational planning and the agency‚Äôs long-term growth ‚Äç Skills & Experience ‚Äç Significant experience in public affairs, strategic communications or political advisory roles within an agency Strong understanding of UK politics and policy, with well-developed networks across the political and stakeholder landscape Proven track record of managing senior client relationships and delivering strategic counsel Demonstrable experience contributing to or leading new business development and commercial growth Experience leading, mentoring and developing teams in a fast-paced consultancy environment Commercial awareness and the ability to balance client delivery with business objectives Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills A collaborative leadership style and a proactive, solutions-focused approach ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For more information on public affairs roles, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Business Development Manager
item.description: The Opportunity: ‚Äç We are partnering with a leading trade and accreditation body to recruit a Business Development Manager. This role focuses on expanding market share by promoting professional training programmes to employers with the capacity to train at scale. You‚Äôll drive new business growth by positioning their brand and qualifications with large or high‚Äëgrowth organisations, identifying fresh opportunities, and converting them into long‚Äëterm training partners. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities: ‚Äç Proactively identify and pursue opportunities to grow the number of clients engaging with our professional training programmes. Map and build relationships with organisations that have the potential to train significant numbers of trainees. Manage prospective client relationships from initial engagement through to onboarding Develop and deliver annual and quarterly account plans Work closely with internal teams and external stakeholders Plan and deliver high‚Äëquality employer engagement and lead‚Äëgeneration events. Track performance metrics to ensure targets are met and exceeded. ‚Äç Key Skills Required: ‚Äç Proven business development experience, ideally in a B2B environment is essential Experience working in practice, private equity, or corporate finance functions, or strong knowledge of professional services and related sectors is desirable. Strong negotiation and contract management skills. Demonstrated success in lead generation and sales conversion. Excellent skills in Word, Excel and PowerPoint, with confidence using CRM systems. A full driving licence and access to a vehicle. ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter forcorporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone whoreports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly andefficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to performat their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Interim Head of Public Affairs
item.description: The Opportunity We are partnering with aleading trade association in the financial services sector to recruit an experienced interim Head of Public Affairs to lead their political and parliamentary engagement at a pivotal time for the financial services sector.This is a 6 month fixed term contract, where you will oversee a high-performing team, manage engagement with parliamentarians, and work across the organisation and with senior industry leaders to deliver meaningful impact. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities Lead, mentor and support the public affairs team to foster an inclusive, collaborative culture Develop and deliver the organisation‚Äôs advocacy strategy and political engagement plans Lead engagement with parliament, government and influential stakeholders Shape policy positions in collaboration with internal policy teams Provide strategic political insight, advice and briefings to senior colleagues Manage senior engagement programmes with member firms Represent the organisation at political and policy events, including conferences ‚Äç Skills and ExperienceRequired Strong experience in public affairs, ideally within the financial services sector Experience in managing a team Excellent understanding of parliamentary and government processes Proven ability to build trusted relationships with political stakeholders Experience designing and delivering successful advocacy strategies ‚Äç Right to Work ‚Äç Applicants must have thelegal right to work in the UK. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Social Media and Content Executive
item.description: The organisation ‚Äç A top UK law firm with a strong international presence, recognised for its high-profile client work and distinctive, market-leading brand. The firm operates across key sectors including innovation, private wealth and real estate, with a reputation for excellence in complex and high-stakes matters. It combines deep sector expertise with a creative, client-focused approach. ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç This top law firm is seeking a Creative Content Executive to join its agile Communications Team, supporting the delivery of high-quality, engaging digital content. Reporting to the Communications Manager, the Creative Content Executive will focus on social media, campaigns and digital storytelling to strengthen the firm‚Äôs brand and market presence. The role offers the opportunity to work closely with marketing, events, digital and business development teams, as well as stakeholders across the firm. ‚Äç Main responsibilities ‚Äç ‚Ä¢ Plan and deliver creative, brand-consistent content and digital campaigns across social media channels including LinkedIn, Instagram and YouTube ‚Ä¢ Manage and grow social media channels, ensuring content is engaging, timely and aligned with wider firm activity ‚Ä¢ Monitor performance and optimise campaigns using analytics tools, providing insight and recommendations ‚Ä¢ Collaborate with internal teams and external agencies to develop content, campaigns and thought leadership ‚Ä¢ Support internal communications and advise colleagues on digital presence, including LinkedIn and video content. ‚Äç Key skills and experience ‚Äç ‚Ä¢ Proven experience in social media, digital content or communications roles ‚Ä¢ Strong understanding of social media platforms, trends, formats and audience engagement ‚Ä¢ Experience creating digital content, including graphics and video, using tools such as Adobe Creative Suite or similar ‚Ä¢ Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to work across teams and stakeholders ‚Ä¢ Strong organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines. ‚Äç Application ‚Äç Please apply below by 2 nd April. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Account Director / Senior Account Director (Technology)
item.description: The Organisation Our client is a fast-growing, independent communications agency specialising in technology, investment, and purpose-led organisations. Now in its fourth year, the agency has built a strong reputation for working with ‚Äúmeaningful‚Äù organisations across climate, femtech, DEI, financial inclusion, health, and education, alongside some of the most ambitious companies shaping the future of technology. The business has grown revenue by 30% over the last financial year. With a growing international footprint, including expansion into New York, the agency supports a diverse client base spanning start ups,scale ups, investors, and institutions across multiple global markets. Its approach is grounded in thoughtful storytelling, high-quality media engagement, and a genuine respect for journalism. The Opportunity This is a key hire within a high-performing and values-driven agency at a pivotal stage of growth. You will take ownership of key client relationships from the outset, leading delivery across accounts while managing and developing a team. Alongside client work, you will contribute to the broader direction of the business, including input into strategy, operations, and new business activity. The role offers significant scope for impact, responsibility, and progression within a collaborative and intellectually curious environment. Main Responsibilities Lead the strategic direction and delivery of multiple client accounts Build trusted, senior-level relationships with clients across sectors Develop and deliver media strategies that generate high-impact coverage Drive proactive media engagement and maintain strong journalist relationships Manage, mentor, and develop junior team members Contribute to business growth through new business and organic account development Support wider agency initiatives across operations and strategy Skills & Experience Demonstrable experience managing client relationships within a PR agency environment Strong background in technology or technology-adjacent communications Proven ability to secure high-quality, top-tier media coverage Experience leading and developing teams Excellent written and verbal communication skills Strong understanding of UK, US, and European media landscapes Interest in purpose, emerging technologies, including AI, and their broader impact Commercial awareness with the ability to identify growth opportunities Wider Benefits One-month paid sabbatical after four years‚Äô service Flexible wellbeing support, including fitness or therapy subsidy Generous annual leave, including additional time over the Christmas period and a birthday day off Regular team activities and company-wide events Monthly development check-ins and access to external training Home office and technology provision Access to learning and wellbeing platforms Health-related benefits including eye care support Close To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For more information on communications roles at leadingagencies, contact Tasmin at Tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporateaffairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutiquesearch firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reportsto them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. Our purpose is to create important and enduringrelationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversityacross our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to performat their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if yourequire any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerationsshould they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Public Affairs Manager
item.description: The organisation Directors UK is the professional association for UK screen directors, representing over 8,000 members across film and television. As a collective management organisation, it negotiates rights deals and distributes royalties, while also championing the creative and economic interests of directors. Directors UK works closely with government, regulators and industry bodies to influence policy, protect rights and support a thriving and inclusive creative sector. The opportunity Directors UK is seeking a PublicAffairs Manager to support and deliver its public affairs strategy at a pivotal time for the screen industries. Reporting to the Director of Communications andPublic Affairs, the Public Affairs Manager will play a key role in engaging government, parliamentarians and stakeholders on issues including copyright,AI, and the freelance economy. This is a hands-on role in a small, collaborative organisation, offering the opportunity to shape policy activity, build relationships and support high-profile engagement across the creative sector. ‚Äç Main responsibilities Support the delivery of Directors UK‚Äôs publicaffairs strategy, working closely with the Director of Communications and Public Affairs Monitor political and policy developments,providing insight, horizon scanning and recommendations on emerging issues and opportunities Draft briefings, consultation responses and policy submissions, building a strong evidence base to support key priorities Map and engage stakeholders across government, parliament and industry, supporting relationship building and external engagement Work with the communications team to align public affairs activity with wider campaigns, content and stakeholder messaging. Key skills and experience Work with the communications team to align public affairs activity with wider campaigns, content and stakeholder messaging. Experience in a public affairs, government relations or policy role Strong understanding of UK government and parliamentary process Experience drafting briefings, consultation responses and policy documents Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present complex issues clearly Strong stakeholder management skills and a proactive approach to identifying opportunities. ‚Äç Application Please apply below. The deadline for applications is Wednesday 15 th April. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Email Marketing Officer
item.description: ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield are partnering with a youth advocacy charity who work closely with teachers, celebrities and government to advocate for a greater focus on physical activity and wellbeing in schools. The charity is looking for an Email Marketing Officer on a10-month maternity cover basis. In this role you will lead marketing email campaigns to help raise the profile of the charity whilst increasing engagement and uptake of services. Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Manage the day-to-day email marketing calendar. Prepare email marketing data for targeted marketing emails using the CRM, ensuring correct personalisation of emails. Create and send centralised marketing emails and newsletters including use of dynamic content, segmentation techniques, A/B testing. Monitor email marketing trends whilst supporting ideas for the charity‚Äôs online marketing campaigns. Monitor email activity to ensure open, click to open and unsubscribes rates remain within the organisation‚Äôs agreed parameters. Report on email engagement rates to understand which customers are most and least engaged with our email activity. ‚Äç ‚Äç Experience ‚Äç Experience of planning, creating and delivering targeted email marketing campaigns. Hands-on experience using a marketing automation platform (e.g. Account Engagement Email (Pardot), Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Mailchimp, Dot Mailer or similar). Skilled in audience segmentation for targeted communications within a regulated or data-sensitive environment. Confident managing marketing data within a CRM system, including data cleansing and de-duplication.¬∑ Proven record of monitoring and reporting on campaign performance metrics. Knowledge of email marketing best practice, including segmentation, automation, personalisation, A/B testing, andoptimisation techniques. Understanding of email performance metrics and how to interpret data to inform improvements. ‚Äç ‚Äç Please send your CV and cover letter to naomi.b@ellwoodatfield.com Application deadline Thursday 16th April. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Public Affairs & Policy Manager (Wales) - Not-For-Profit (Health Sector)
item.description: About the Role ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is partnering with a leading not-for-profit organisation in the health sector to appoint a Public Affairs & Policy Manager. This is a key role leading policy and external affairs activity in Wales, with collaboration across UK-wide teams. ‚Äç The successful candidate will lead public affairs strategy, manage stakeholder relationships, and provide expert political and policy advice to senior leadership. You will oversee a small team and play an important role in shaping the organisation‚Äôs external engagement and policy positioning. ‚Äç This role offers the opportunity to influence health policy, build strong stakeholder relationships, and contribute to meaningful impact across the healthcare system. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç You will lead public affairs and policy activity in Wales, providing strategic insight and advice to internal and external stakeholders. You will build and maintain strong relationships with policymakers, government bodies, and partners across the health sector, ensuring the organisation is positioned as a credible and influential voice. ‚Äç You will provide high-quality political intelligence, policy analysis, briefings, and advice to senior leaders. The role will involve overseeing policy projects, consultations, stakeholder engagement, and external communications activity, while managing and supporting a small team to deliver high-quality work. Working closely with colleagues across the UK, you will contribute to a joined-up external affairs strategy and help ensure the organisation responds effectively to developments in the political and policy environment. ‚Äç ‚Äç About You ‚Äç We are looking for someone with a strong understanding of the Welsh political, public affairs, and health policy environment. You will have experience providing policy and strategic advice to senior stakeholders and be confident in building trusted relationships with senior external partners. ‚Äç You will bring excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce high-quality briefings and policy documents. You will have experience managing complex workstreams and delivering projects to a high standard, as well as the ability to lead or support a team. Welsh language skills are desirable but not essential. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç How to Apply ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via Ellwood Atfield. Suitable applicants will be invited to an initial briefing call with our team to discuss the role in more detail. ‚Äç Closing date: Monday 27 April 2026 Early applications are strongly encouraged , as shortlisting and interviews will take place on a rolling basis. ‚Äç ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Senior Campaign and Donor Experience Manager
item.description: ‚Äç The organisation: ‚Äç ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to partner with Alzheimer's Research UK, the leading charity fighting to ensure a cure for dementia whilst championing research to revolutionise the way dementia is diagnosed and treated. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity: This is an exciting role at Alzheimer‚Äôs Research UK, created at a time when donations from philanthropic partnerships have grown substantially. The Philanthropy and Partnerships directorate has an impressive record of securing seven-figure donations and winning significant corporate partnerships with noteworthy brands such as Omaze, Cadbury, Warburtons, Roadchef and Dobbies. ‚Äç The charity is looking to build upon this success and have launched a new fundraising campaign - Roadmap to a Cure - designed to drive further growth and income generation from major donors, trusts, foundations and corporate partners. ‚Äç As a Senior Campaign and Donor Experience Manager, you will oversee the successful delivery of the campaign, managing an impressive team of experts across Proposition Management, Stewardship and Prospect Development. You will drive accountability and ambition within the team; working closely with the Director of Philanthropy and Partnerships to lead on the operational aspects of the Appeal and support your team in engaging a wide array of funders. ‚Äç ‚Äç Responsibilities ‚Äç Collaborate with theDirector of Philanthropy and Partnerships as well as the Heads of CorporatePartnerships and Philanthropy to lead on the implementation of the strategy for the Roadmap to a Cure campaign. Create and implement the strategy for the High Value Experience and Development Team. Design and review clear KPIs whilst monitoring progress against these to drive accountability and ambition for the campaign. Ensure the Philanthropy and Partnerships Directorate has the proper tools to be data driven in its decision making and fundraising. Promote a culture of collaboration across the High Value Experience and Development team and other teams across the charity, to develop a culture of innovation, accountability and resourcefulness. Line manage three members of staff, including the Donor Relations Manager, Proposition Manager and Prospect Development Manager: coaching and empowering the team to achieve their operational plans and priorities. Cultivate strong ties across the charity to ensure all work delivered by the team is informed by strategic and operational decisions and that colleagues provide the information and access required to deliver high quality relationship management, prospect research, stewardship and propositions. Lead delivery of the operations elements of the Campaign. Ensure the delivery of a compelling and robust case for support, a suite of impactful fundraising materials, a strong prospect pool, a series of cultivation and stewardship events, and a stewardship matrix which can be flexed to meet the needs of different types of donors. Keep up to date with ARUK‚Äôs research and other news to be able to engage effectively with supporters, communicating the charity‚Äôs key messages both among the team and to key supporters. ‚Äç ‚Äç Person specification: ‚Äç Demonstrable knowledge and experience of major gift/and or corporate fundraising. Knowledge of fundraising appeals and overseeing significant campaign projects. Proven record of formulating fundraising strategies, including ensuring the operational success of a significant philanthropic appeal. Adept in creating engaging donor-facing materials. Experience in conducting prospect research and donor pipeline management. Ability to foster strong professional ties and influence senior members of staff. Highly motivated and target driven, with an ability to demonstrate a proactive and pragmatic approach and identify ways to deliver creative and innovative solutions. Excellent communicator, with an ability to present arguments clearly to a diverse audience and generate a collaborative working environment. Experience managing a sizeable team, with an ability to inspire, motivate and challenge team members. ‚Äç ‚Äç Staff benefits ‚Äç Flexible working arrangements ‚Äìtwice a week in the Cambridge Office. 29 days of annual leave with an option to buy or sell 5 holiday days a year. Personal growth development plans for individual staff members. Private medical insurance, as well as a health cash plan, life insurance and income protection. ‚Äç How to apply: To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: Friday 24 April, 10:00am ‚Äç ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Communications and PR Manager
item.description: ‚Äç ‚Äç About the organisation: ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to partner with the National Centre for Universities and Business (NCUB) who serve as the collective voice of its members, comprising the UK‚Äôs universities and leading businesses. ‚Äç NCUB is committed to bringing organisations together to leverage the skills and talent fostered by UK universities and to further strengthen thenation‚Äôs world-class research and development. Its strategic, cross-sector network links universities, businesses, and governments together to provide a platform for members to engage on areas of shared opportunity. NCUB is a trusted expert, driven by data. ‚Äç Through strong relationships with UKRI and government, NCUB acts as a voice of influence, ensuring its members‚Äô views are heard. Now, more tha never, the UK needs the shared learning and skills that arise from collaboration. This approach will help build a stronger nation, with a flourishing economy, a united society, and lasting positive impact. ‚Äç The opportunity: ‚Äç NCUB are seeking an experiencedCommunications and PR Manager to lead their communications function and strengthen its visibility, reach and influence. This is a strategic and hands-on role, focused on ensuring NCUB‚Äôs work engages key audiences including policymakers, senior leaders across business and higher education, and the media. You will develop a proactive, media-focused approach that raises the organisation‚Äôs profile and positions it as a credible voice in national discussions on innovation, skills and economic growth. ‚Äç Working closely with the SeniorLeadership Team, you will shape messaging, support external engagement and build strong relationships with journalists and stakeholders. You will also oversee the creation of high-quality content across digital and communications channels, ensuring a clear and consistent voice. ‚Äç This is an exciting opportunity to take ownership of a critical function and play a leading role in how a small, ambitious organisation influences thinking and delivers meaningful impact at the heart of the UK‚Äôs innovation ecosystem. ‚Äç Main responsibilities: ‚Äç ‚Äç Lead and deliver NCUB‚Äôs communications strategy to maximise reach, impact and visibility. Develop and implement a proactive PR approach, building strong media and stakeholder relationships. Drive media engagement, securing coverage and thought leadership opportunities while supporting senior leaders. Oversee digital and content channels, using data and AI tools to improve performance and engagement. Manage brand, website and content, evaluate impact, support stakeholder communications and line manage the Events Manager. Line manage three members of staff, including the Donor Relations Manager, Proposition Manager and Prospect Development Manager: coaching and empowering the team to achieve their operational plans and priorities. Cultivate strong ties across the charity to ensure all work delivered by the team is informed by strategic and operational decisions and that colleagues provide the information and access required to deliver high quality relationship management, prospect research, stewardship and propositions. Lead delivery of the operations elements of the Campaign. Ensure the delivery of a compelling and robust case for support, a suite of impactful fundraising materials, a strong prospect pool, a series of cultivation and stewardship events, and a stewardship matrix which can be flexed to meet the needs of different types of donors. Keep up to date with ARUK‚Äôs research and other news to be able to engage effectively with supporters, communicating the charity‚Äôs key messages both among the team and to key supporters. ‚Äç Key skills and experience required: ‚Äç Proven experience delivering strategic communications and PR activity that raises organisational profile and secures impactful media coverage. Strong ability to translate complex issues into clear, compelling messages, with excellent copywriting skills across multiple formats. Demonstrated expertise in using digital, social and traditional channels to drive engagement, alongside data-led evaluation of communications performance. Experience identifying and securing media opportunities, building relationships with journalists, and using monitoring tools to track impact. ‚Äç ‚Äç Benefits: ‚Äç Salary banding: ¬£45,000 - ¬£50,000, dependent on experience Hybrid working (2 days per week in the office) Office based in London (SE1 6FE) 30 days including 3-day Christmas closedown plus Bank Holidays Enhanced Maternity and Paternity pay Employer pension contribution of 5% (on top of the 5% employee contribution) Rolling annual training and development plan Benenden Healthcare scheme. ‚Äç ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‚Äç The deadline for applications is Friday 17 April 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director (Crisis)
item.description: ‚Äç The Organisation ‚Äç Our client is a well-established strategic communications consultancy. They work across a range of sectors, supporting clients through complex and often high-profile situations. ‚Äç The Opportunity ‚Äç This is a senior role within a growing and ambitious practice, offering the opportunity to shape and develop crisis and issues communications capabilities. The successful candidate will play a key role in leading client work, contributing to the broader growth of the practice, and defining areas of specialism within a generalist offer. ‚Äç Main Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead the delivery of crisis and issues communications programmes for a range of clients Provide senior strategic counsel on reputational risk, litigation, and high-profile issues Support the development and positioning of the crisis practice within the wider business Build strong, trusted relationships with clients and key external stakeholders Oversee and develop account teams, ensuring high-quality delivery Contribute to new business activity, including pitches and proposals Maintain high standards across client work, ensuring consistency and efficiency Mentor and support the development of junior colleagues ‚Äç Skills & Experience ‚Äç Strong experience in crisis, issues, and/or litigation communications (essential) Excellent understanding of the media landscape and handling of sensitive situations Proven ability to advise senior stakeholders in complex environments Demonstrated leadership capability with a collaborative approach Commercial awareness, with experience supporting business development activity Ability to operate with sound judgement in fast-paced, high-pressure situations ‚Äç Benefits ‚Äç Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package Flexible and hybrid working arrangements Generous leave and wellbeing support Structured learning and development opportunities Inclusive, collaborative culture with regular social and team activities ‚Äç To Apply ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For more information on communications roles, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Advocacy Manager
item.description: The Opportunity ‚Äç Water UK is looking to recruit an Advocacy Manager - acritical role supporting the organisation‚Äôs growing political engagement, campaign delivery and policy influencing work. ‚Äç This is a fast-paced and varied role at the centre of a nationally significant agenda. You will report to the new Head of Advocacy and work closely with senior colleagues to support high-profile campaigns and help shape Water UK‚Äôs political engagement. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Monitor the political landscape and regulatory developments, providing timely analysis and advice Support the planning and delivery of advocacy campaigns across key strategic priorities, alongside the Head of Advocacy and Director of Public Affairs Coordinate engagement with senior stakeholders at the highest level, both internally and externally, and produce high-quality materials including speaking notes and stakeholder briefings Collaborate with the wider public affairs, policy, communications and member engagement teams to ensure coherent and aligned advocacy activity ‚Äç Key Skills & Experience Required ‚Äç Strong understanding of UK politics and parliamentary processes, with prior experience working in advocacy, public affairs or similar role Excellent written skills with the ability to produce clear and persuasive material, with strong attention to detail Proactive and able to manage multiple priorities with limited oversight ‚Äç To apply ‚Äç We have partnered with Ellwood Atfield on the appointment of this role. If this role is of interest, please apply with your CV and Covering Letter via the link below - we welcome early applications
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Speechwriter
item.description: Ellwood Atfield is partnering with a leading mission-driven not for profit to find an exceptional Speechwriter. This is a rare opportunity to craft inspiring, high-stakes speeches for a CEO and senior leadership team - translating cutting-edge research and science into narratives that move audiences. ‚Äç You'll bring: ‚Äç Outstanding storytelling skills, making complex science compelling and accessible Ideally a strong portfolio across formats and audiences and speechwriting experience The ability to adapt voice and tone for different leaders and contexts ‚Äç This role sits at the heart of a communications and advocacy team doing work that genuinely matters. ‚Äç Interested? Please apply via the link on this advert, for more info please speak to Josh at Ellwood Atfield ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Lead Internal Communications Manager
item.description: The opportunity: Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to be partnering with a leading professional body to recruit a Lead Internal Communications Manager. This is an influential role at the intersection of culture, people, and communications, ensuring colleagues are informed, engaged and aligned with the organisation‚Äôs strategy and values. ‚Äç This role is well‚Äësuited to someone who thrives in a busy environment, brings structure and focus to complex organisations, and is highly skilled at translating information into clear, purposeful communications. You will act as the organisational lead for internal communications, working closely with senior leaders and the People team to drive engagement, support change, and build a strong, informed internal culture across the organisation. ‚Äç Main responsibilities: ‚Äç Develop and deliver an internal communications strategy aligned to organisational priorities Lead employee engagement activity, including all-staff events, leadership briefings, staff forums, and employee voice initiatives Advise senior leaders on communications, culture, change, values, and people-related messaging Oversee and improve internal communication channels, including the intranet, while creating clear, high-quality content Use employee insight to shape communications, manage reputational risk, maintain the annual communications calendar, and line manage one team member ‚Äç Key skills and experience required: ‚Äç Proven experience developing and delivering internal communications strategies and campaigns Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to translate complex information into clear, engaging messages Strong stakeholder management skills, with confidence advising and influencing senior leaders Experience managing and improving internal communications channels, engagement, and change initiatives Strong organisational skills, sound judgement, and the ability to balance priorities, risk, and business impact ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. The deadline for applications is Friday 24 April 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield are the leading headhunters for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Research & Policy Executive
item.description: ‚Äç The organisation ‚Äç The Global Infrastructure Investor Association (GIIA) is the leading global body representing private infrastructure investors, fund managers and advisors. Its members manage over $2 trillion across energy, transport, digital and social infrastructure, supporting investment in systems that underpin economic growth, connectivity and the transition to net zero. GIIA operates at the intersection of industry and policy, working with governments and stakeholders to create environments that enable sustainable and resilient infrastructure. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç GIIA is seeking a Research & Policy Executive to join its Policy and Public Affairs team, supporting the development of high-quality research and evidence-led policy work. This is an excellent opportunity to build experience across global markets, contributing to work spanning the UK, Europe and the US. The Research & Policy Executive will play a key role in strengthening GIIA‚Äôs research capability, supporting policy development and engaging with members on key infrastructure issues. ‚Äç ‚Äç Main responsibilities ‚Äç Conduct research and data analysis on infrastructure policy, investment and market trends across priority regions Monitor political and policy developments, producing clear insight and summaries for internal and external audiences Support the development of evidence-based policy positions, including drafting briefings, reports and consultation responses Maintain and develop internal datasets and evidence bases, ensuring accuracy and analytical rigour Support member engagement through working groups, research projects and policy-focused events. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key skills and experience ‚Äç Degree, or equivalent, in politics, economics, international relations or a related field Strong research and analytical skills, with the ability to synthesise complex information High attention to detail and confidence working with data, including Excel Strong written and verbal communication skills Interest in infrastructure, investment and public policy, with an understanding of political and policy processes. ‚Äç ‚Äç Application ‚Äç Please apply below. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director (Healthcare)
item.description: ‚Äç The Organisation Our client is an independent strategic communications consultancy with an international footprint and a strong reputation for delivering high-impact, insight-led work. They advise high-profile organisations and senior leaders across complex, regulated sectors. ‚Äç The firm combines the agility of a boutique with the capability to deliver integrated communications, public affairs, and reputation management programmes at scale. Their culture is collaborative, entrepreneurial, and grounded in delivering measurable commercial outcomes. ‚Äç ‚Äç The Opportunity This is a senior leadership role within a growing healthcare and life sciences practice, with a particular emphasis on biotech. The Director will lead strategic communications programmes for clients operating at the forefront of scientific innovation, while also playing a key role in expanding the practice through new business development. The role requires strong engagement with tier 1 media and the ability to provide senior counsel on high-stakes reputational and regulatory issues. You must have agency experience for this role. ‚Äç ‚Äç Main Responsibilities Lead and oversee strategic corporate communications programmes for healthcare and life sciences clients, with a strong focus on biotech Provide senior counsel on reputation, positioning, issues management, and crisis preparedness in complex, regulated environments Build and maintain relationships with tier 1 media, securing high-impact coverage and shaping narratives Develop trusted, long-term client relationships at senior level, acting as a key strategic advisor Drive new business activity, including identifying opportunities, leading pitches, and converting prospects Collaborate with public affairs specialists to advise on policy, regulatory, and political engagement Lead, mentor, and develop high-performing teams, fostering a collaborative and ambitious culture Contribute to the growth and strategic direction of the healthcare practice ‚Äç Skills & Experience Must have agency experience Significant experience in healthcare communications, with strong exposure to biotech Demonstrable track record of working with tier 1 media and delivering impactful media strategies Proven ability to generate and convert new business opportunities Deep understanding of the regulatory, scientific, and reputational landscape in life sciences Experience advising senior stakeholders in complex or sensitive situations Strong leadership capability, with experience managing and developing teams Commercially minded, with the ability to grow client accounts and contribute to practice expansion Excellent written and verbal communication skills ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‚Äç ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Head of Consumer Communications
item.description: The Opportunity If you are motivated by scale, visibility, and the chance to shape how a nation engages with a brand,this role offers something genuinely distinctive. ‚Äç This organisationoperates one of the UK‚Äôs most widely recognised consumer products, reachingmillions of people every week through a vast national network. With annualrevenues in the billions and a significant contribution to public good causeseach year, it combines commercial scale with meaningful societal impact. ‚Äç As Head of ConsumerCommunications, you will step into a high profile leadership role at a pivotalmoment. The function has recently been reshaped and continues to evolve,creating a genuine opportunity to define its future direction. ‚Äç You will lead how thebrand shows up in culture, driving creativity, modernising approach, andbuilding stronger in house capability. This is not about maintaining the statusquo. It is about raising ambition, unlocking new ideas, and increasing relevancein a fast moving media landscape. ‚Äç This is a role with realvisibility, complexity, and scope, offering the opportunity to make a lastingimpact on a nationally significant brand. ‚Äç Main Responsibilities Lead and develop the consumer communications function, overseeing a team of around 10 across multiple workstreams Deliver high impact, national storytelling across products, people, and purpose led initiatives Drive a more modern, integrated approach to PR and social, improving creativity and effectiveness Reduce reliance on agencies by building stronger in house capability and ownership Act as a senior strategic partner within the wider communications function Build and maintain strong relationships with national media, securing high profile coverage across print, broadcast, and digital Operate at pace, managing multiple priorities while maintaining quality and consistency Lead through change, evolving team structure, capability, and ways of working ‚Äç Key Skills and Experience Required Proven experience leading consumer communications within a major, recognisable brand such as retail, FMCG, drinks, or similar Strong track record of delivering high volume, high impact media coverage at a national level Deep understanding of modern PR, including integration across social, digital, and earned media Excellent media relationships across national press and broadcast Creative thinker with strong storytelling instincts and the ability to generate culturally relevant ideas Hands on leadership style with the ability to balance strategy and execution Experience building and leading teams, improving performance, and driving pace Comfortable operating in complex, evolving environments with ambiguity and change Experience managing agencies, with a clear view on when to use them versus in house delivery ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link. Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Events Executive
item.description: About the organisation ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is delighted to have partnered with a respected trade association who are looking for an Events Executive on a permanent basis. The organisation is renowned for working alongside Government and business leaders to provide insights and advice around securing investment opportunities in Asia. The organisation work closely with its members to help businesses influence policy, connect with decision-makers and identify opportunities for growth. ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç The trade association are looking for a proactive Events Executive with a strong eye for detail to play a key role in delivering high impact events across the UK. This is an exciting opportunity for someone looking to advance their career in a fast-moving environment. In this role, you will be responsible for supporting end-to-end event planning to create experiences for members which will help strengthen stakeholder and business relationships. This includes supporting the planning and delivery of notable events such as high-level roundtables, conferences, networking opportunities and seminars. The role incorporates the full life cycle of event co-ordination, including concept development, planning and logistics, as well as marketing support and follow-up engagement. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Support the development of event concepts, timelines, budgets, and planning documents to ensure smooth end to end delivery. Coordinate venue bookings, supplier negotiations, and vendor contracts to secure cost effective and high quality event services. Liaise with external partners to ensure all logistics including catering, equipment, and hospitality. Oversee administrative tasks such as delegate registration, speaker coordination, and preparation of event materials and resources. Provide onsite operational support to ensure events run smoothly and meet expected standards. Assist with event communications, including drafting invitations, managing mailing lists, and supporting promotional activities. Support post event follow up, including updates to systems, sending thank you notes, and consolidating feedback from participants. Contribute to post event reporting and evaluations to improve future event delivery and impact. ‚Äç Key Skills and Experience Required ‚Äç Strong written and verbal communication skills and confident in engaging with a range of stakeholders. Strong resilience and time management skills; able to work proactively. Proficient in Canva and Microsoft Office, whilst being comfortable with AI tools ‚Äç Benefits ‚Äç Salary banding ¬£28,000 - ¬£30,000 dependent on experience Hybrid working arrangement ‚Äì 3 days in the London office ‚Äç How to apply ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. The deadline for applications is Thursday 7th May 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Associate Director (Financial Services)
item.description: The Organisation ‚Äç Our client is a well-established, mid-sized communications agency with a global offering and a strong heritage in financial services. Known for its agile and collaborative culture, the agency has built a reputation for delivering high-quality, insight-led work, alongside industry recognition for its people-first approach. ‚Äç The Opportunity ‚Äç This is a pivotal role within a high-performing financial services team, acting as the number two to the Head of Financial Services. You will play a central role in shaping client strategy, leading delivery across key accounts, and supporting the continued growth of the practice within a nimble and ambitious agency environment. ‚Äç Main Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead the strategic direction and delivery of integrated communications programmes for financial services clients Act as a senior counsel to clients, building trusted and long-term relationships Support and deputise for the Head of Financial Services as required Drive new business activity, including contributing to pitches and identifying growth opportunities Oversee and mentor junior team members, fostering a high-performing and supportive team culture Ensure consistently high standards of delivery across multiple accounts Collaborate across the wider agency to deliver integrated, multi-market campaigns Skills & Experience ‚Äç Significant experience within a communications agency, with a focus on financial services Strong understanding of the financial services landscape, including regulatory and reputational considerations Proven ability to lead client relationships and provide senior strategic counsel Experience in managing and developing high-performing teams Track record of contributing to business development and agency growth Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, agile environment Close ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‚Äç For more information on communications roles, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com. ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Head of Communications
item.description: The organisation ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield are proud to be partnering with the Aerospace Technology Institute (ATI). They are based in London and responsible for the UK‚Äôs Aerospace Technology Strategy. ATI promote transformative technology in air transport, representing a joint industry and Government commitment to R&D funding of over ¬£4 billion by 2031. The ATI represents a single, coherent national aerospace technology development programme that aims to strengthen the links between industry and academia whilst maximising opportunities for UK industry to gain access to European R&T programmes. ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç As Head of Communications at the ATI, the successful candidate will direct and elevate the organisation's reach and impact across UK and global audiences, shaping how the world understands ATI's role at the forefront of aerospace technology. ‚Äç Leading the Communications Team, the Head of Communications will own and evolve the full channel mix from media relationships and thought leadership to flagship events including the ATI Annual Conference, while delivering a Communications Plan that brings to life ATI's impact, sector vision, and technology leadership. The Head of Communications will work closely with partners, manage high-profile media engagements, and have regular exposure to the ATI Executive Management Team and Board, with occasional involvement in international events. ‚Äç Reporting to the Chief Relationships Officer, the postholder will directly manage a communications team of 3 and oversee a team of 4, providing strong leadership, clear direction, and a culture of continuous improvement driven by data and feedback. As a member of the Extended Leadership Team, the Head of Communications will collaborate across the ATI to align internal and external messaging, championing ATI's values of Collaborating for Success, Independent Leadership, and Forward Thinking. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Establish and deliver a clear Communications plan that maximises the visibility, impact and reach of ATI content. Deliver an ATI events programme that supports delivery of the ATI funding programme, development of sector competitiveness and growing UK market share by 2035. Transform the delivery of ATI Programme information to ensure portfolio, case study and impact data is shared in a way that maximises accessibility and utility. Work closely with Programme partners, the media and recipients to ensure co-ordinated and timely communications and proactively seek to resolve misunderstanding or misinformation. Provide guidance and content for the delivery of ATI internal communications. Line manage, develop and support the Communications Team, contribute as part of the extended Leadership Team and be a role model in the organisation. ‚Äç Experience required ‚Äç Proven experience running strategic communications campaigns in complex, multi-stakeholder environments, with the ability to structure insights and evidence into compelling narratives quickly and accurately. Demonstrable understanding of the economic and political landscape of the UK aerospace sector or UK Government, with the ability to translate this into strategic opportunities and actionable implications. Experience negotiating and managing contracts, alongside a track record of successfully coordinating multiple delivery partners. Experience working and delivering in multi-stakeholder environments, with the ability to build relationships at all levels and anticipate and respond to a range of perspectives. Ability to create and commission high-quality content and collateral, combined with strong organisational skills to lead and motivate colleagues to deliver to time and budget. Proven experience managing and developing a team, setting clear goals and priorities, fostering a collaborative culture, and driving performance to deliver high-quality outcomes. Direct experience of working in the UK civil aerospace sector, UK Government or a communications agency would be desirable but not essential. ‚Äç Benefits ‚Äç Hybrid working (2 office days minimum per week in the London office - W1T) Attractive pension scheme Bupa Private Medical Insurance Discretionary bonus scheme Paid membership to one professional body per year 25 days annual leave, plus bank holidays and additional Christmas leave Enhanced maternity and paternity leave EV scheme available ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below by the 19th May, 2026 and include a copy of your CV. ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunters for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not don
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Digital Senior Account Manager
item.description: The Organisation ‚Äç Our client is a fast-growing strategic advisory firm operating at the intersection of communications, policy and reputation. With an international footprint, the firm supports senior leaders across government, business and high-profile organisations navigating complex, high-stakes environments. The team is known for its discreet, insight-led approach, combining geopolitical awareness with clear strategic execution. ‚Äç The Opportunity ‚Äç This is an exciting opportunity for a digitally focused communications professional to join a high-performing and entrepreneurial team. You will play a key role in delivering integrated campaigns and digital strategies for clients operating in complex and often high-profile contexts.
