The role offers significant international exposure, opportunity for regular travel and the chance to work on high-impact mandates with senior stakeholders. ‚Äç Main Responsibilities ‚Äç Plan and execute digital communications strategies across sensitive, high-profile client accounts Manage day-to-day social media activity, including drafting, scheduling and publishing content for senior individuals and organisations Develop digital narratives that support reputation management, public positioning and campaign objectives Monitor online conversations, media coverage and stakeholder sentiment, providing real-time insight and recommendations Support crisis and issues management through rapid-response digital content and messaging Conduct digital and stakeholder research, including audience mapping and platform analysis, to inform strategy Analyse campaign performance and produce insight-led reports to refine and optimise activity Work closely with colleagues across strategy, research and public affairs to deliver integrated client programmes Provide hands-on support to senior clients, ensuring digital output is accurate, timely and aligned with wider communications objectives Monitor political, regulatory and media landscapes to inform campaign activity ‚Äç Skills & Experience ‚Äç Experience in digital communications within an agency, in-house or public sector environment Strong understanding of social media, digital campaigning and online engagement strategies Ability to operate in fast-paced, high-pressure environments with sound judgement Experience supporting senior stakeholders or working on campaigns involving complex or sensitive issues Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights Interest in geopolitics and international affairs Excellent written and verbal communication skills Likely to have 3‚Äì5 years‚Äô relevant experience ‚Äì this will not be suitable for someone more experienced and there is no flexibility here ‚Äç To Apply ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‚Äç Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Head of Public Policy & Research
item.description: The organisation ‚Äç People‚Äôs Partnership is a leading financial services organisation, responsible for supporting more than seven million savers across the UK, with over ¬£40bn Assets Under Management (AUM). It plays a significant role in the pensions landscape, working to improve financial outcomes and deliver long-term value for its members. The organisation is recognised for it strong commitment to innovation, customer focus and driving positive change across the industry. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity ‚Äç People‚Äôs Partnership is seeking a Head of Public Policy& Research to lead its thought leadership, research, campaigns and public policy agenda at a senior level. This role will shape how the organisation influences government, industry and key stakeholders, ensuring its voice is clear, credible and impactful across the pensions and financial services landscape. Working closely with senior leadership, including the Head of Public Affairs, Head of Policy and executive team, the Head of Public Policy & Research will set the strategic direction for research, corporate narrative, campaigns and external positioning. ‚Äç ‚Äç Main responsibilities ‚Äç Lead the development and delivery of a strategic thought leadership and policy research programme aligned to organisational priorities Set and own the corporate policy narrative, ensuring clear and consistent positioning across external policy audiences and internal stakeholders Oversee the commissioning and delivery of high-quality research and insight to support policy development and industry influence Build and maintain senior relationships across industry, government, think tanks and partner organisations Lead and develop a high-performing team, ensuring delivery of impactful campaigns, content and public policy outputs. ‚Äç Key skills and experience ‚Äç Senior experience in policy, research, public affairs or corporate affairs within financial services, government, a think tank or membership organisation Strong track record of developing thought leadership and influencing policy and industry debate Experience commissioning and translating research into clear, compelling narratives for external audiences Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to engage and influence at senior level Strong communication skills, with experience producing high-quality briefings, reports and strategic content. ‚Äç ‚Äç ‚Äç Application Please apply below by Monday 18th May. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Faculty Communications Manager (Humanities and Social Sciences)
item.description: The organisation: ‚Äç Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to partner with Queen Mary University of London, a prestigious Russell Group University located in the heart of East London. Ranked 31st in the world for the quality of their research, and with a long-standing history of commitment to social justice and academic excellence, Queen Mary is consistently ranked among the top universities in the UK and globally. ‚Äç With a diverse and inclusive community of students and staff from over 170 countries, the University fosters an environment that encourages innovation, collaboration, and critical thinking across a very wide range of disciplines. ‚Äç ‚Äç The opportunity: ‚Äç The role of Faculty Communications Manager (Humanities and Social Sciences) sits within the central Communications team and serves as the primary communications lead for the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences. ‚Äç As one of three Faculty Communications Managers‚Äîeach aligned with one of the University‚Äôs faculties‚Äîthis role is responsible for developing and delivering strategic internal and external communications for the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences. Working closely with Faculty leadership, academic and professional services staff, the postholder supports the University‚Äôs broader goals of increasing awareness and reputation, driving student recruitment, and enhancing engagement among staff and students. ‚Äç The Faculty Communications Manager (Humanities and Social Sciences) is the lead contact for communications across the Faculty and works in close partnership with the Faculty Marketing Manager to be a point of contact to the central marketing and communications services. The role is supported by the central Communications team and directly line manages a team of two communications officers. ‚Äç Main Responsibilities ‚Äç Lead and deliver integrated internal and external communications strategies for the Faculty, supporting reputation, engagement (internal and external) and student recruitment objectives. Act as the principal communications adviser to Faculty leadership teams, providing expert guidance on media relations, campaigns, stakeholder communications and reputation management. Develop high-quality content across multiple channels, including press releases, digital platforms, social media, newsletters and leadership communications, ensuring consistency of brand and messaging. Monitor, evaluate and report on communications activity and campaign performance, using insight and analytics to inform future strategy and continuous improvement. ‚Äç Key Skills and Experience required: ‚Äç Significant experience of developing and implementing successful communications activities and campaigns (including media relations activities). Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with experience translating complex subject matter into engaging and accessible content for diverse audiences. Proven experience of coordinating integrated communications across traditional and digital platforms to meet marketing and communications goals. Comfortable working alongside senior leadership teams and or Boards/Committees. Experience of working in within higher education or a complex organisation. Experience of matrix managing and directly line managing others Proven ability to meet tight deadlines ‚Äç Benefits: ‚Äç Salary banding: ¬£54,617 ‚Äì ¬£60,901, dependent on experience* Hybrid working (3 days per week in the office) Office based in London (E1 4UJ) 30 days holiday plus bank holidays *Please note that Queen Mary advertise salaries on a range to indicate the trajectory of progression that can be made. Appointments are usually made at the start of the salary range. The Queen Mary salary structure includes automatic pay progression within the published grades, subject to service, funding and performance. In addition to this, there are performance related annual pay review schemes in place. ‚Äç How to apply: ‚Äç To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. The deadline for applications is Tuesday 19 th May 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Senior Director of Executive Communications
item.description: ‚Äç Role Purpose Lead and shape the executive voice of a global organisation, driving strategic communications and employee engagement across Europe. ‚Äç The Senior Director, Executive Communications is responsible for shaping, protecting and amplifying the voice of the CEO and senior leadership across Europe. This role sits at the intersection of strategy, storytelling, employee engagement and digital communications, translating enterprise priorities into clear, compelling narratives that drive alignment, trust and performance. ‚Äç The role is both strategic and highly operational, owning end to end delivery of executive communications, from narrative development and drafting through to digital execution, sequencing and distribution. ‚Äç Key Responsibilities ‚Äç Set and steward the CEO and enterprise narrative, ensuring clarity, consistency and relevance across all channels and moments that matter. Draft and edit high‚Äëstakes communications on behalf of the CEO and ELT, including organisational announcements, leadership notes, speeches, townhalls, blogs and event messaging. Act as a trusted advisor to senior leaders, providing counsel on tone, timing, positioning and stakeholder impact. Lead enterprise‚Äëwide employee communications, translating strategy, transformation and performance into messages that resonate with colleagues. Own leadership‚Äëled employee moments such as town halls, leadership updates and milestone communications, ensuring they are engaging, data‚Äëdriven and authentic. Partner closely with People, Strategy and Leadership teams to reinforce culture, purpose and change. Drive a digital‚Äëfirst approach to executive and employee communications, using platforms such as Engage, intranet, video and social to extend reach and relevance. Modernise executive communications through smart use of visuals, links, data, multimedia and new formats, ensuring content is concise, scannable and shareable. Ensure messages are adapted appropriately for different channels while maintaining a single narrative spine. Own the end‚Äëto‚Äëend communications flow for executive and enterprise priorities, including sequencing, timing and coordination across internal, external and digital channels. Work seamlessly with Corporate, Product and Market Communications teams to ensure alignment and avoid fragmentation. Partner with global counterparts to localise and execute global narratives effectively in Europe. Act as a final editorial gatekeeper for executive communications, ensuring accuracy, consistency and readiness before distribution. Manage sensitive, confidential communications with discretion and sound judgement. Anticipate reputational risk and advise on mitigation through proactive messaging. Key Skills and Experience ‚Äç Extensive experience in executive, employee or enterprise communications within a complex, matrixed organisation. Exceptional writing and editing skills, with the ability to shift seamlessly between strategic narrative and sharp, concise execution. Strong understanding of digital communications and channel strategy, with a track record of modernising leadership communications. High emotional intelligence and confidence operating with senior leaders. Proven ability to manage multiple high‚Äëstakes workstreams under pressure without losing quality or judgement. ‚Äç Application ‚Äç To apply, please do so below, or contact Jamie Ferguson at jamie.f@ellwoodatfield.com. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Policy and Public Affairs Lead (Wales)
item.description: The organisation Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with a leading health charitythat plays a prominent role in shaping policy and delivering high-profilecampaigns to improve access to diagnosis, treatment and support for patientsacross the UK. The opportunity Our client is looking for an experienced Policy and Public Affairs Lead to leadthe development and communication of evidence-based policy positions acrossWales. In this role, you will play a central part in shaping and deliveringinfluencing strategies, while building and maintaining strong relationshipswith stakeholders across the Welsh Government and wider health sector. You will work closely with researchers, healthcareprofessionals and external partners to amplify policy and campaign activity,ensuring the charity‚Äôs priorities are informed by robust evidence and capableof driving meaningful change. This is an exciting opportunity to join a highlyregarded organisation at a time of significant policy focus and campaigningactivity across Wales. ‚Äç Main responsibilities Work closely with colleagues across the organisation to ensure alignment on policy and campaign priorities. Draft high-quality policy materials including briefings, consultation responses, reports and other written outputs on key policy areas. Ensure policy development is backed by a strong evidence base, informed by healthcare professionals and patient insight across Wales. Monitor the political and healthcare landscape in Wales to identify emerging risks, opportunities and influencing priorities. Collaborate with external organisations on areas of mutual interest to maximise campaign reach and impact. Work closely with public affairs colleagues to identify and deliver activities that support the charity‚Äôs influencing objectives. Build and maintain effective relationships with policymakers, healthcare leaders, clinicians and external partners across Wales. Represent the charity at key events and conferences, acting as a source of expertise on the Welsh political and healthcare landscape. Support the press and PR team to ensure policy and public affairs priorities are reflected in media activity and opportunities. ‚Äç Key skills and experience required Experience developing and implementing policy and influencing strategies within a Welsh political environment. Strong ability to utilise data and evidence, including patient insight, to support policy development and influencing activity. Proven track record of delivering public affairs activity across Wales, with the ability to influence senior external stakeholders. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to represent the charity externally and communicate complex policy issues clearly. Strong project management skills, with the ability to work independently while managing multiple priorities and stakeholders. ‚Äç Benefits ‚Äç Salary banding between ¬£36,500 - ¬£38,000 Hybrid working, with 3 days per week in the Cardiff office 25 days annual leave, plus an additional day for each full year worked (up to 30 days) ‚Äç How to apply ‚Äç To apply, please submit your CV via the link below. ‚Äç The deadline for applications is Tuesday 26th May 2026. However, we may close the advert earlier if we receive a high volume of applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Graduate Communications Intern (6-month FTC)
item.description: The Opportunity: ‚Äç We are partnering with a mission driven organisation in the insurance industry to recruit a Graduate Communications Intern on a 6-month FTC. This is a great opportunity to immerse yourself in a highly collaborative team dedicated to addressing critical challenges around risk management. This full-time internship offers hands-on experience in internal, corporate and social and digital communications, in a purpose driven organisation. ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Accountabilities: ‚Äç Collaborate with the communications team to develop and deliver a new internal communications strategy. Support planning and execution of internal events. Maintain the corporate calendar, including ensuring Chair, CEO and Exec speaking engagements are captured and advise the team when they need to prepare speeches and briefings. Collate content for monthly round-up emails. Produce content for social and digital channels Lead on admin responsibilities for the communications team ‚Äç ‚Äç Key Experience Required: ‚Äç Great Writing Skills: Contribute to internal announcements, blogs, and corporate content with clarity and impact. Excellent Interpersonal and Communication Skills: Collaborate effectively with team members and stakeholders while maintaining professionalism. Detail-Oriented and Organised: From managing SharePoint pages to tracking events, demonstrate your ability to juggle multiple responsibilities seamlessly. Adaptability: Embrace changing technologies and business needs with confidence and resourcefulness. Proactive Problem Solver: Take initiative and work independently while integrating seamlessly into our client‚Äôs collaborative environment. Experience producing content for Social and Digital channels ‚Äç ‚Äç If this role looks of interest, please apply through the link in this advert and not via email. ‚Äç
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield
